topics.titleTr: Cursor mu Offset mi: API Sayfalama
topics.angle: comparison + why deep pages are slow and how keyset fixes correctness
topics.brief: Data-led: why page 5000 is slow. Sections: offset (LIMIT/OFFSET scans and discards rows) vs keyset/cursor (WHERE (col,id) > (...) ORDER BY col,id); the correctness bug where deletes shift rows across pages so users skip or duplicate records; a ~17x speedup at a million rows; when offset is fine (small/static admin data). Key claims to verify: OFFSET 99980 scans ~100k rows before returning 20; keyset avoids skipped/duplicated rows during inserts and deletes; ~17x faster at 1M rows in Postgres. Primary sources: Sequin keyset-cursors-not-offsets post; Gusto API-pagination guide. End with a keyset query template and a cursor-encoding note.

topics.angle: deep-dive + which level you actually need and the retries it forces
topics.brief: Question-led: which isolation level do you actually need? Sections: read anomalies (dirty read, non-repeatable read, phantom, lost update); the four SQL levels (read uncommitted to serializable); what Postgres vs MySQL default to; MVCC and the serialization failures you must retry. Key claims to verify: Postgres defaults to Read Committed while many assume higher; Postgres Repeatable Read uses snapshot isolation and prevents phantoms; Serializable can abort transactions and needs retry logic. Primary sources: PostgreSQL transaction-isolation docs; Kleppmann 'Designing Data-Intensive Applications' isolation chapter. End with a level-vs-anomaly matrix and a retry pattern.

topics.angle: deep-dive + publishing events atomically with your database write
topics.brief: Story of a lost payment event. Sections: the dual-write problem (DB commit succeeds, broker publish fails, or vice versa); writing events to an outbox table inside the same transaction as the business change; a relay that publishes them (polling vs log-based CDC with Debezium); idempotent consumers under at-least-once delivery. Key claims to verify: you cannot atomically write a DB and publish to a broker without a pattern like this; log-based CDC gives low-latency relay with no extra DB load; consumers must dedupe. Primary sources: microservices.io transactional-outbox; Debezium outbox-event-router docs. End with a schema and a relay checklist.

topics.angle: how-to + picking a chunking method and defaults that actually retrieve
topics.brief: Data-led playbook. Sections: fixed vs recursive vs semantic vs agentic chunking; recommended defaults (256-512 tokens, 10-20% overlap); recursive 512-token splitting as a strong 2026 baseline; semantic chunking for unstructured prose; late chunking with long-context embeddings. Key claims to verify: a Feb 2026 benchmark ranked recursive 512-token splitting first across seven strategies; semantic chunking lifted accuracy to ~71% vs fixed-size baselines on one dataset; agentic chunking is highest quality but slow and expensive. Primary sources: Firecrawl best-chunking-strategies 2026; Extend semantic-chunking best practices. End with a strategy-selection flowchart and an eval harness note.

topics.titleTr: Küçük Dil Modelleri Ne Zaman Yeter?
topics.titleEn: When Small Language Models Beat Big Ones
topics.angle: contrarian + you are overpaying for a frontier model on narrow tasks
topics.brief: Contrarian: you're overpaying for a frontier model. Sections: SLMs (a few million to ~7B params) win on cost, latency, privacy, and on-device deploy; where a fine-tuned 7B beats a generalist on narrow, repeatable tasks; where big models stay necessary (broad knowledge, open-ended reasoning); the routing pattern (a small model handles most requests and escalates hard cases). Key claims to verify: fine-tuned SLMs are faster/cheaper/more accurate on repeatable workloads; routing plus drafting is the 2026 best practice; frontier models remain needed for unpredictable reasoning. Primary sources: index.dev SLM-vs-LLM 2026 reality check; MachineLearningMastery SLM guide. End with a build/route decision checklist.

topics.titleEn: LLM Observability: Tracing Your AI Calls
topics.angle: how-to + instrumenting an LLM app with OpenTelemetry GenAI conventions
topics.brief: Field notes: debugging an agent you can't see. Sections: why request/response logging isn't enough (chains, tool calls, retries); OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions (gen_ai.* attributes for model, tokens, tool calls, finish reason); tracing with Langfuse as an OTel/OTLP backend; wiring evals, cost, and latency dashboards. Key claims to verify: the field converged on OTel gen_ai.* as the vendor-neutral baseline; Langfuse ingests OTLP and self-hosts for data residency (now part of ClickHouse); token/cost/finish-reason attributes are standardized. Primary sources: opentelemetry.io GenAI-observability 2026 blog; Langfuse OpenTelemetry docs. End with a minimal instrumentation snippet.

topics.titleEn: From IC to Tech Lead: The First 90 Days
topics.angle: guide + a 30-60-90 plan for the shift from writing code to leading
topics.brief: Story of a strong IC's rocky first quarter. Sections: the identity shift (impact through others, not commits); a 30-60-90 plan (listen and map, then voice observations around week 4-6, then lead changes); building a wide network (skip-level, peer leads, cross-functional partners); letting go of being the best coder. Key claims to verify: coding stops being the primary job and this surprises most new leads; the shift from listening to leading lands around weeks 4-6; first-90-days relationships become a multi-year network. Primary sources: LeadDev 30-60-90 new-manager plan; First Round 90-day plan. End with a fill-in 30-60-90 template.

topics.titleEn: How to Make Better Engineering Estimates
topics.angle: how-to + decomposition, ranges over point estimates, and calibration
topics.brief: Field notes on estimates that blew up. Sections: why estimates fail (unstated assumptions, ignored unknowns, optimism bias); relative sizing vs hours; ranges and confidence over single numbers; breaking work down until each item is boring; tracking estimate-vs-actual to calibrate over time. Key claims to verify: decomposition reduces estimation error; communicating a range beats false-precision point estimates; historical calibration improves forecasts more than heroics. Primary sources: Steve McConnell 'Software Estimation: Demystifying the Black Art'; team retrospectives on estimate accuracy. End with an estimation checklist and a phrase-bank for pushing back on false precision.

topics.titleEn: Software Supply Chain Security: SBOM to SLSA
topics.angle: checklist + SBOMs, signing, and build provenance wired into CI
topics.brief: Question-led: could a poisoned dependency reach your prod? Sections: the threat (cumulative malicious packages past 1.2M; attacks doubled in 2025); SBOMs enriched with VEX instead of paper inventories; artifact signing with Sigstore (cosign, Fulcio, Rekor); build provenance via SLSA; wiring these checks into CI. Key claims to verify: Sonatype counted 450k+ new malicious packages in 2025; Sigstore's cosign/Fulcio/Rekor are production-ready and widely adopted; SLSA provides a verifiable source-to-artifact chain of custody. Primary sources: Cloudsmith 2026 supply-chain guide; OpenSSF Sigstore docs. End with a CI hardening checklist. No real secrets in examples.

topics.titleTr: Helm mi Kustomize mi: K8s Yapılandırma
topics.titleEn: Helm vs Kustomize for Kubernetes Config
topics.angle: comparison + templating vs overlays and where mature teams land
topics.brief: Data-led comparison for real fleets. Sections: Helm (versioned charts, Go templating, releases, rollbacks, huge chart ecosystem) vs Kustomize (base plus overlays, no templating, built into kubectl); Helm 4 (server-side apply, WASM plugins) and Kustomize v5.8 interop; the common convergence (Kustomize for first-party apps, Helm for third-party charts). Key claims to verify: Helm 4 shipped at KubeCon NA Nov 2025 with native SSA and a WASM plugin system; Kustomize is built into kubectl and templating-free; mature teams mix both. Primary sources: Spacelift Kustomize-vs-Helm; Tasrie 'we manage 100+ clusters' 2026. End with a decision table and a hybrid setup.

topics.titleTr: Tauri mi Electron mu? 2026 Masaüstü Kararı
topics.titleEn: Tauri vs Electron in 2026: Which Should You Pick?
topics.angle: comparison + weigh Tauri's native-WebView+Rust footprint against Electron's mature Chromium ecosystem for real 2026 apps
topics.brief: Outline: architecture (bundled Chromium vs OS WebView + Rust core); size/RAM (~3MB vs 150MB+); security capability model; ecosystem gap (Electron ~1.66M weekly npm vs Tauri ~85k); Tauri v2 desktop+mobile from one frontend; when Electron still wins. Claims to verify: size/RAM multipliers, plugin registry 47→120+ (Jan 2025–Apr 2026), download counts. Primary sources: https://v2.tauri.app/ and https://www.electronjs.org/

topics.titleTr: ESLint ve Prettier'e Veda: Biome ve Oxlint
topics.titleEn: Biome vs Oxlint: Rust Tooling Replaces ESLint
topics.angle: comparison + guide teams migrating from ESLint+Prettier to Rust-based Biome or Oxlint/Oxfmt, with a rule-coverage vs speed tradeoff
topics.brief: Outline: why JS tooling is being rewritten in Rust (esbuild/swc precedent); Biome as zero-config all-in-one (v2.4, 450+ rules, ~97% Prettier compat) vs OXC/Oxlint speed champ (50-100x ESLint) that runs alongside ESLint; greenfield vs legacy migration paths; plugin-depth caveats. Claims to verify: rule counts, 50-100x speed, 97% Prettier compat. Primary sources: https://biomejs.dev/ and https://oxc.rs/

topics.titleTr: DuckDB mi SQLite mı? Gömülü Veritabanı Rehberi
topics.titleEn: DuckDB vs SQLite: Which Embedded Database?
topics.angle: comparison + explain when to reach for analytics-focused DuckDB vs transactional SQLite, and how they compose in a 2026 local-first stack
topics.brief: Outline: OLAP (columnar DuckDB) vs OLTP (row-store SQLite); 'SQLite for analytics' framing; aggregation speed (10-100x); a realistic pipeline (SQLite at edge/mobile syncing, DuckDB querying Parquet), plus DuckDB reading SQLite/Postgres directly; edge-SQLite platforms (Turso, D1, LiteFS). Claims to verify: 10-100x aggregation delta, Turso embedded-replica sync, postgres_scanner. Primary sources: https://duckdb.org/ and https://motherduck.com/learn/duckdb-vs-sqlite-databases/

topics.titleTr: Kubernetes'siz Deploy: Kamal ve Coolify Rehberi
topics.titleEn: Deploy Without Kubernetes: Kamal vs Coolify
topics.angle: comparison + help solo devs and small teams ship to their own VPS with Docker-based PaaS tools instead of Kubernetes or pricey clouds
topics.brief: Outline: why teams are dropping K8s and Heroku-style bills; Kamal (37signals, YAML+SSH, kamal-proxy, zero platform overhead) vs Coolify/Dokploy (dashboard PaaS) vs Dokku; SSL/domains/databases/rollbacks; a decision matrix by team size. Claims to verify: Kamal's proxy-only footprint, Coolify feature set, none using Kubernetes. Primary sources: https://kamal-deploy.org/ and https://coolify.io/

topics.titleTr: Apache Iceberg Nedir? Lakehouse'un Yeni Standardı
topics.titleEn: What Is Apache Iceberg? The Lakehouse Standard
topics.angle: deep-dive + explain why Iceberg became the default open table format and how the lakehouse replaces warehouse+lake duplication
topics.brief: Outline: what an open table format is; Iceberg vs Delta vs Hudi; store-once/query-many (Spark, SQL, Python, ML) without lock-in; catalog governance and V4 spec; who's adopting (Apple, Snowflake, Databricks). Claims to verify: Gartner lakehouse upgraded to 'transformational', Iceberg Summit 2026 ~600 attendees/70+ sessions. Primary sources: https://iceberg.apache.org/ and https://www.snowflake.com/en/blog/engineering/iceberg-summit-2026-recap-v4-spec/

topics.titleEn: WebAssembly Beyond the Browser: WASI in 2026
topics.angle: deep-dive + show how WASI 0.3 and the Component Model turned Wasm into a real server/edge runtime and where it beats containers
topics.brief: Outline: Wasm's jump from browser to server; WASI 0.2 Component Model + interface types; WASI 0.3 native async I/O (Feb 2026) closing the concurrency gap; runtimes (Wasmtime, Spin, Cloudflare Workers, Fastly); cold-start wins vs containers; plugin/sandboxing use cases. Claims to verify: WASI 0.3.0 async futures/streams, 10-40x cold-start vs containers, CNCF '70% evaluate/use Wasm outside browser'. Primary sources: https://wasi.dev/ and https://thenewstack.io/wasi-1-0-you-wont-know-when-webassembly-is-everywhere-in-2026/

topics.titleTr: Zig 1.0 Geliyor: C'nin Yerini Alabilir mi?
topics.titleEn: Zig in 2026: A Real C Replacement?
topics.angle: deep-dive + introduce Zig as a pragmatic 'better C', contrast it with Rust's safety model, and assess the coming 1.0
topics.brief: Outline: Zig's goal (replace C, not out-safety Rust); comptime metaprogramming, no hidden control flow, best-in-class cross-compilation and drop-in C/C++ compiler; where it's used; what 1.0 stability locks in. Claims to verify: TIOBE ~#39 (Apr 2026), 64% most-admired in SO survey, latest stable 0.15.2 (Oct 2025), 1.0 expected mid-late 2026. Primary sources: https://ziglang.org/ and https://survey.stackoverflow.co/2025/technology

topics.titleTr: Yapay Zeka Tarayıcıları: Comet, Atlas ve Gemini
topics.titleEn: AI Browsers Compared: Comet, Atlas and Gemini
topics.angle: comparison + map the agentic-browser landscape, what agent mode actually does, security risks, and why Atlas's shutdown is a cautionary tale
topics.brief: Outline: what an agentic browser is (scroll/click/fill/multi-step); standalone (Comet, Atlas) vs bolt-on (Chrome Gemini, Edge Copilot); a capability/pricing table; sidebar-spoofing and prompt-injection security risks; the ChatGPT Atlas wind-down as a product-churn lesson. Claims to verify: Atlas shutdown date (~Aug 9 2026), Comet free + iOS launch Mar 2026. Primary sources: https://www.perplexity.ai/comet and Webrazzi Atlas coverage
